title: Method and apparatus for current measurement in polyphase electricity supply
abstract: A measurement circuit is arranged to make several measurements, either at different times or in respect of different frequency components of currents measured by current sensors in respective phases of a multiphase supply system. The measurments are then used to correct for discrepencies in the transfer function of the sensors.
claims:
Claims 1. A current measurement apparatus for use in a multiphase electricity supply comprising at least two current measurement transducers, and a data processor arranged to determine ratios of transfer functions of the at least two current measurement transducers by comparing plural measurements of current and determining the ratio of the transfer functions by application of Kirchoffs law of current flow. 2. The current measurement apparatus of claim 1 , in which each transfer function is determined differently from the or each other transfer function ratio using at least one of a) real and imaginary parts of a measured current flow over at least one frequency range; and b) current flow measurements made at different times. 3. The current measurement apparatus of any preceding claim, in which the current measurement apparatus performs a frequency domain analysis, and uses measurements of in phase (real) and out of phase (imaginary) components of current at one or more frequency ranges as part of the determination of the ratio of transfer functions. 4. The measurement apparatus of claim 3, in which signals from the measurment transducers are transformed from the time domain to the frequency domain to provide destimates of the real and imaginary parts of the measured current at a plurality of frequency bins, and the data processor is arranged to solve a plurality of independent equations in which for two or more frequency bins the sum of real parts of the measured curent is zero and the sum of the imaginary parts of the measured current is zero. 5. The current measurment apparatus of any preceding claim, further comprising a characterisation apparatus for characterising the transfer function of at least one of the current measurement transducers to a specified accuracy. 6. The current measurement apparatus of claim 5, in which the characterisation apparatus comprises a current supply for adding a first known time varying current to a current being measured by a first one of the current transducers, and a processor for monitoring output of the first current transducer, for extracting a signal component due to the known time varying current, and on the basis of comparing the extracted signal of known time varying current with the known time varying current to determine a transfer function of the first current sensor. 7. The current measurement apparatus of claim 6, in which the current measurement appartus is further configured to apply a second known time varying current to a second one of the current transducers, and where the first and second known time varying currents can be applied at different times, or the first and second time varying currents have differing characteristics and are applied concurrently. 8. The current apparatus of claim 6 or claim 7, in which the data processor is arranged to subtract a signal corresponding to the load current measured by the first transducer so as to make the contribution due to the known time varying current easier to measure, the load current signal being estimated using knowledge of the transfer functions of other ones of the current sensors. 9. The current measurement apparatus of any preceding claim, comprising first, second and third current transducers for measuring the current in first, second and third phases of a three phase supply or current in a split phase supply. 10. The current measurement appratus of any preceding claim, wherein the data processor is further arranged to determine the current flowing in each phase of the multiphase supply. 11. The current measurment apparatus of any preceding claim, in which the data processor is arranged to perform a time domain analysis, whereing the data processor is arranged to apply plural different time windows to the measured output signals to identify a change in the loading of the multiphase electricity supply. 12. The current measurment apparatus of claim 11 , in which one of the following applies: a) the change in load is expected to take place from cycle to cycle of an AC current, and the time windows span cycles of the AC current; b) the change in load current is expected to take place within a cycle of the AC current and the windows are within one cycle of the AC current. 13. The current measurement apparatus of any preceding claim, included in a power consumption meter including at least one voltage measurement apparatus for measuring a voltage and an accumulator for accumulating a sum of products of voltage at a given time and the current drawn at the same given time. 14. The current measurement apparatus of any preceding claim, in which the data processor is further arranged to determine a measure of confidence in the data used to estimate a transfer function ratio or in an estimate of a transfer ratio and its measure of confidence are used when updating an estimate of the transfer function ratio. 15. The current measurement apparatus of any preceding claim, in which the transducers selected from shunt resistors, current transformers, Rogowski coils, Hall probes, magnetoresistors are: a) all of the same type; or b) comprise different types of transducers. 16. A method of correcting current transducer mismatch in a multiphase supply where a first load current flows in a first supply conductor, a second load current flows in a second supply conductor and a third load current flows in a third supply conductor, the method comprising determining a ratio of the transfer functions of first, second and third transducers measuring the first, second and third load currents, respectively, by assuming that the sum of currents is zero. 17. The method of claim 16, in which a known reference signal is applied to the first current path such that an output of the first transducer comprises a load current component and a reference signal component, and where signals from the second and third transducers are used to estimate and remove the load current component from the first transducer output so as to substantially leave the reference signal component such that the reference signal compnent can be compared with the known refernce signal in order to characterise the transfer function of the first transducer. 18. A current measurement method comprising: measuring by way of a measurement arrangement a load drawn current signal in each of at least three conductors of a multi-phase electricity supply to provide a respective output signal; applying a reference input signal to the measurement arrangement wherein the output signal measured in respect of a conductor comprises a load output signal corresponding to the load drawn current signal in the conductor and a reference output signal corresponding to the reference input signal; 

 determining at least first and second transfer function ratios, the first transfer function ratio being determined in respect of a first pair of conductors and the second transfer function ratio being determined in respect of a second pair of conductors, the at least first and second transfer function ratios being determined in dependence on at least a part of each of output signals measured in respect of the at least three conductors and which lacks a reference output signal, each transfer function ratio being determined differently from the at least one other transfer function ratio in respect of at least one of real and imaginary parts in at least one frequency band and time window of the output signals; and making a determination in respect of at least one of: a measured load drawn current signal; and electrical power consumed by the at least one load, the determination being made in dependence on the at least first and second transfer function ratios, the reference input signal and at least a part of output signals measured in respect of the conductors and comprising reference output signals The current measurement method of claim 18, further comprising removing the reference output signal from the output of a current sensor before using the output to estimate the electrical power consumed by a load. The current measurement method of claim 18 or claim 19, further comprising estimating a load component representing a load current at a sensor output and subtracting the load component so as to leave substantially only the reference output signal.
